lesson.menu_title: He Said, She Said — Reported Speech
lesson.menu_desc: Reported speech (backshift + reporting verbs) + witness-statement vocabulary -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 10 -- He Said, She Said: reporting what witnesses said in court.
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Luiz Bressane | Aula 10 -- He Said, She Said

listenings.text: Good afternoon. This is the court reporter with a summary of this morning's testimony. The first witness stated that she had seen the defendant leave the building at nine o'clock. She added that the side door had been left unlocked. The second witness claimed that he had heard nothing unusual that night. When he was asked about the parked car, he admitted that he had not been paying attention. Counsel for the defense argued that the two accounts could not both be true. The judge replied that the matter would be decided once the transcript had been reviewed. The session was then adjourned until tomorrow.

You are listening to the legal affairs update. In today's hearing, the key witness told the court that she had recognized the defendant immediately. She insisted that her statement had not been changed since the first day. The defense lawyer responded that the witness had given a different account in her earlier deposition. He asked the court why two versions existed. The prosecutor replied that the difference would be explained by a later witness. Reporters were told that a ruling is expected by Friday. We will bring you more as the case develops.
